disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A population bowel-screening test kit arrived by mail today and triggered an acute wave of fear and physical arousal (racing heart, breathlessness, trembling)
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me what happened when the kit arrived?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The bowel screening kit came in the post today and I just went into a panic — my heart started pounding and I couldn't catch my breath.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: First-degree relative (sibling) died of bowel cancer within the last month — recent bereavement driving the fear
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone close to you had bowel problems?
Why does this test frighten you so much?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My brother died of bowel cancer last month. Seeing this kit made me feel like it's my turn now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No bowel symptoms — denies rectal bleeding, denies any change in bowel habit, denies unexplained weight loss
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any bleeding from the back passage?
Any change in your bowel habit or weight you can't explain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no bleeding, my bowels are normal, and my weight hasn't changed. I feel physically fine, I'm just terrified.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No cardiac-type chest pain — denies crushing, central, or radiating chest pain; the chest sensation is the pounding/tightness of the acute episode and eases as she calms
trigger_question_examples: Is there any tight or crushing pain in the centre of your chest?
Does the chest feeling spread to your arm or jaw?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's not a crushing pain — it's my heart racing and a tight, breathless feeling, and it eases off when I sit and breathe slowly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Asymptomatic and screening-eligible (within the invited age range); has never done this test before and is unsure of the collection technique
trigger_question_examples: Have you done one of these home tests before?
What worries you most about doing it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've never done one before and I don't really understand how you're supposed to collect the sample. I'm worried I'll do it wrong — or that it'll say I've got cancer.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone, recently bereaved, no prior episodes of this kind and no known mental-health diagnosis; at risk of avoiding screening altogether
trigger_question_examples: How have you been coping since your brother passed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm on my own tonight. Since my brother died I've been all over the place, and honestly part of me just wants to throw this kit in the bin and forget about it.

patient_initiated_exchanges.trigger_condition: Patient asks whether the kit means she already has cancer
patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Does getting this kit mean they already think I've got what my brother had?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure that the kit is a routine population-screening invitation sent to everyone in the eligible age range, not a response to any finding about her; explain screening detects early changes before symptoms; validate the grief without diagnosing.

patient_initiated_exchanges.trigger_condition: Patient asks if she is having a heart attack
patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: My heart is going so fast — am I having a heart attack?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Take the fear seriously and screen for cardiac red flags (crushing/central/radiating chest pain); if absent and symptoms are easing with calming, reassure without over-escalating, but give a clear chest-pain 000 safety-net.
